{{Quote|Very well, ''[…]'' The judgment of C'baoth is that Svan will pay to Tarm the full wages agreed upon. ''[…]'' The judgment will be carried out immediately.|Dark Jedi Joruus C'baoth|<ref name="Dark" />}}
'''Tarm''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] villager residing on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Jomark/Legends|Jomark]] under insane [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Joruus C'baoth]]'s rule.<ref name="Dark" /> In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]],<ref name="DarkDate">''[[Dark Force Rising]]'' states that it takes place five [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[4 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. Therefore, ''Dark Force Rising'' must take place in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]</ref> during [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]], [[Svan (Jomark)|Svan]], another Human villager, attempted to [[Death/Legends|kill]] Tarm with a [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugthrower]] and Tarm attempted to defend himself with a [[Knife/Legends|knife]]; however, C'baoth and a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who he was [[Jedi training/Legends|training]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], arrived after they sensed the "evil" in the building the villagers' confrontation was taking place in in and stopped it. Ultimately, C'baoth considered Tarm in the right following his and Svan's motives, and punished Svan with [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] and ordered him to pay Tarm the full wages they had set. Tarm had [[Color/Legends|dark]] skin and a [[Hair/Legends|beard]].<ref name="Dark" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tarm first appeared in the [[1992]] novel ''[[Dark Force Rising]]'', the second installment in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|''Thrawn Trilogy'']].<ref name="Dark" /> He was later pictured in [[Dark Force Rising 3|''Dark Force Rising'' 3]], the third comic issue in ''Dark Force Rising''{{'s}} comic adaptation, the story was written by [[Mike Baron]] and the artwork was penciled by [[Terry Dodson]].<ref name="DH">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/95-160/Star-Wars-Dark-Force-Rising-3-of-6|text=STAR WARS: DARK FORCE RISING #3 (OF 6)}}</ref>
